content:
✔ How Fate Manipulations Work:
• A fate shift (wheel manipulation, divine intervention, or cosmic bargain) can be triggered by a player.
• To undo a fate shift, one player from each faction must come forward and agree to sacrifice something.
• The sacrifices must be significant and thematically tied to their faction.
• If no one steps forward, the fate stands permanently.

✔ Example Faction-Based Sacrifices:
• The Scholar’s Offering → Must surrender knowledge (ex: losing a bonus, giving away an answer to another player).
• The Warrior’s Tribute → Must take a lasting debuff (ex: lowered attack rolls, temporary loss of abilities).
• The Trickster’s Gambit → Must accept random punishment instead of a predetermined one.
• The Devout’s Atonement → Must take on another player’s burden or debuff as penance.

📜 Why This Works:
✔ Prevents a single person from reversing fate—requires teamwork.
✔ Ensures that factions remain engaged in power struggles.
✔ Adds weight to faction identity—each has a unique cost.
